campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. They also discussed plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant money to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarship fund
New rules extending interim ordinance 2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant applications for bridge planning and construction

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a new rule (ordinance) the city is considering. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the usual first-reading delay, planning to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on new city ordinance
Council waived the first reading requirement to speed up the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Residents had opportunity to speak before council proceeding with the rule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they reviewed and approved meeting minutes from previous sessions in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details were cut off in the agenda provided.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Review of closed session discussions from March

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to organize their upcoming meeting schedules. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for April 21st, then looked ahead to plan the April 28th agenda, deciding what items to include and what time slots to assign to different topics. The council also reviewed official records from early April and discussed a VEBA Advisory Committee matter.
keyTopics: Finalized April 21, 2026 meeting agenda
Planned April 28, 2026 draft agenda items
Set specific time slots for agenda discussions
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee discussion

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026, where they discussed the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including which infrastructure and improvement projects to fund. They also recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week with official proclamations to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget and capital improvement projects funding decisions
Teen Month proclamation recognizing local youth
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting notes from March and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The council introduced a new ordinance that would update Chapter 19.71 of the city's rules governing how landlords and renters are treated.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 25, 2026
Updates to residential tenant protections program
Changes to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code
New ordinance introduced affecting landlord-tenant regulations

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents had the chance to speak about any city issues for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections to choose new leadership positions (Chair and Vice Chair) and discussed the city's budget plans for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any local matter
Election of new Chair and Vice Chair for the council
Fiscal year budget presentation and discussion
Note: Council cannot make decisions on topics not officially scheduled on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 covered important legal information about how residents can challenge city decisions. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from when that decision is finalized to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, unless a different deadline applies.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to legally challenge city decisions
Legal challenges governed by California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6
What counts as a 'quasi-adjudicative decision' that can be challenged
How residents can protect their rights if they disagree with city actions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters and planning decisions. The main focus was updating the city's five-year housing and community development plan, reviewing requests from individual council members for new projects or studies, and considering changes to how the city fills council vacancies.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a more important historic resource, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and making the building earthquake-safe.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building getting upgraded historic status
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Downtown Commercial zone development incentive
Historic Resources Board meeting schedules and assignments
March 12, 2026 meeting minutes approval
